action_class_identifiers.summary: Read or surface current release, operator, archive, trust, or receipt state without authoritative mutation.
Describe intended work, readiness, or review posture without authoritative mutation.
Exercise dry-run, preflight, or simulation semantics without claiming live completion.
Check admissibility, completeness, or compatibility without necessarily performing live mutation.
Perform scoped control-plane or support mutation without crossing public-commitment, trust-root, or live-runtime cutover boundaries.
Execute only under explicit human review because continuity, visibility, or rollback consequences are too high for routine mutation.
Execute only under privileged operator posture because the action changes publication, trust, licensing, or comparable external commitment meaning.
Remain blocked or forbidden under current maturity even if a tool can describe the action.

evaluation_dimension_identifiers.summary: How much release, operator, archive, trust, or user-visible state can be affected.
Whether the action is reversible, partially reversible, or effectively irreversible.
What canonical receipt and continuity evidence must exist before and after the action.
Whether the action changes identity, visibility, selection, or only inspection posture.
Whether the action depends on or changes admissibility relative to release contract profile and resolution law.
Whether the action changes retention, visibility, availability, or continuity interpretation.
Whether the action changes external commitment, authenticity posture, or rights posture.
Whether lifecycle, replay, snapshot, isolation, or coexistence assumptions are implicated.
Whether lawful manual/automation parity and meaningful rehearsal exist.
Whether execution requires review, privileged approval, or remains blocked.

receipt_provenance_requirement_categorie.summary: Inspection may emit support evidence without implying full operator transaction continuity.
Rehearsal or dry-run must remain explicitly non-live in its evidence shape.
Validation posture must preserve what was checked and under which policy surface.
Live governed operator or release-control mutation must emit canonical receipt-bearing evidence.
High-impact actions must preserve linkage into later archive, rollback, visibility, and review history.
Some actions cannot lawfully proceed if the required continuity evidence cannot be preserved.

runtime_sensitivity_categories.summary: No meaningful lifecycle, replay, snapshot, isolation, or coexistence consequence.
Touches release selection and compatibility without directly asserting live runtime replacement.
Touches retention, visibility, or continuity interpretation while remaining primarily control-plane scoped.
Interacts with lifecycle, replay, snapshot, isolation, or coexistence assumptions and requires caution.
Would imply live cutover or restartless runtime replacement and therefore remains heavily gated.
Would imply authority handoff or distributed state transition and therefore remains beyond the current safe execution envelope.

safety_policy: The execution envelope refines release/control-plane posture without creating new permission.
Prohibited safety posture remains prohibited even if a tool can technically express the action.
operator_and_receipts: Execution posture must preserve typed operator transaction meaning.
Receipt class and continuity expectations must remain visible across rehearsed, blocked, and live outcomes.
release_doctrine: Execution posture cannot replace release identity, compatibility truth, or selection truth.
Publication, trust, and licensing gates remain upstream on external commitment posture.
runtime_doctrine: Rehearsal does not imply live cutover approval.
Release-control execution does not prove hotswap or distributed authority readiness.

canonical_surfaces: upstream safety, operator, receipt, release, runtime, and checkpoint doctrine
governed execution decisions where later doctrine explicitly defines them
canonical_vs_derived_distinctions.rule: Derived surfaces may expose or summarize execution posture but may not redefine it.

ambiguities_eliminated: task or exposure does not equal execution authorization
rehearsal does not equal live permission
release-control actions are not all equal in risk
tool convenience does not define execution posture
downstream_refinement_prompts: later guarded reassessment of Phi-B4 and Phi-B5
later publication and trust operational doctrine
